author: Sharma, Devi D.
imprint: (Studies in Tibeto-Himalayan languages, 1). Delhi: Mittal Publications
title: A descriptive grammar of Kinnauri
notes: JP 7/14/97:  It's not clear how entry has been done.  Forms were entered in alphabetical order by gloss, but there is not comprehensive word list per se in the volume.  Instead, there are a number of small word lists throughout the volume, used to illustrated different aspects of the language.  It looks like body parts were found, checked off in the volume, put in a list, alphabetized by gloss, and then entered.  Transcriptions match (with the small "exception" that the retroflex dot appears over 'n' in the volume, under in the database).  Some morpheme boundaries have been inserted into entered forms.  Due to the disconnected nature of the data, the number of total forms is difficult to estimate.  It looks like about 10ɹ of the forms have been entered, which would yield a total of about 1000 forms, but this is only a *very* rough estimate.
